title: kalmia polifolia var. microphylla
intro: Kalmia polifolia var. microphylla, also known as the mountain laurel or bog laurel, is a variety of shrub that belongs to the heath family and is native to eastern North America. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of tannins and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Kalmia polifolia var. microphylla have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, sore throats, and digestive problems, with benefits including reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, Kalmia polifolia var. microphylla is often used as a tincture or infused in water as a tea, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
